vBRIEFInfo.description: Bug in skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md Phase 2 Step 1: the dual-source fetch rule does not cover the cold-start case where the agent's first fetch lands before the bot reviewer (Greptile) has posted, leading to false-clean exit declarations on freshly-opened PRs.

plan.title: Review-cycle dual-source fetch can miss late-arriving bot reviews

narratives.Problem: Phase 2 Step 1 of the review-cycle skill mandates dual-source fetching (`gh pr view --comments` + MCP `get_review_comments` or `gh api` fallback) and `do_not_summarize_output: true` to catch the 'Comments Outside Diff' section. Both correct. The gap: if the agent's first fetch lands BEFORE the bot has posted (e.g. T+30s after PR creation; Greptile lands at T+3-7min), both sources return zero findings. The Step 6 exit condition then evaluates against an empty review and declares 'no P0/P1 remaining' even though the bot review is still in flight. The Step 4 polling loop covers any cycle that includes a push, but the cold-start case (first review-cycle invocation on a freshly-opened PR with no fixes yet) is uncovered.
narratives.Overview: Add a `~` SHOULD rule to Phase 2 Step 1 mandating a re-fetch after a ~60s delay when the initial dual-source pass returns no bot review on the current HEAD SHA, before the Step 6 exit condition can be evaluated. Companion `⊗` MUST NOT rule against declaring exit on a single empty fetch.
narratives.Background: Bot reviewers (Greptile in deft's setup) typically land within 3-7 min of PR creation/push. The user's global rule (`KthiAoZEVMVffm0rv4vxQ6`) already documents this gap qualitatively ('re-check after initial fetch if bot reviewers like Greptile are configured'). This work codifies it into directive's deterministic tier. The poller template (templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md) handles the same case correctly via its loop body -- the gap is in the one-shot review-cycle entry path, not the poller.
narratives.Constraint: MUST be skill prose addition (not code) -- the review cycle is agent-driven, no script to wire. MUST live in Phase 2 Step 1, before Step 6 exit-condition evaluation. MUST cite the global-rule precedent so the deft-internal version is the canonical source. MUST NOT change the poller template (correct already) or the polling loop (Step 4) which already handles this for push-driven cycles. SHOULD match the existing prose tone (`~`/`!`/`⊗` legend per RFC 2119 subset).
narratives.Risk: Very low. Skill prose change only; no code; no tests on logic, only content tests on the skill file. The existing polling loop (Step 4) guarantees safety for any cycle that pushes a fix; this addition covers the orthogonal cold-start case.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) Phase 2 Step 1 of skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md gains a `~` SHOULD rule and a `⊗` MUST NOT rule covering the late-arriving bot review case. (2) Cross-references the user-rule precedent and the poller template (no-op for the poller; positive guidance for the one-shot entry path). (3) Optionally: the same rule is restated in meta/lessons.md as a one-line cross-reference (per the [AXIOM] strongest-applicable-layer rule, the rule body lives in the skill, not duplicated in prose). (4) CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] / Fixed.
narratives.Test: tests/content/test_review_cycle_skill.py (existing; extended OR new module if it doesn't exist): regex-assert the new `~` rule presence, the `⊗` rule presence, and the canonical phrasing tokens (`60s`, `re-fetch`, `before evaluating`). Failure messages cite file + missing pattern.
narratives.Action: 1. Edit skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md Phase 2 Step 1 to add the `~` and `⊗` rules per the issue body's verbatim text. 2. Cross-reference the user rule + poller template for context. 3. Add tests/content/test_review_cycle_skill.py (or extend existing) with regex assertions on the new rule tokens. 4. CHANGELOG [Unreleased] / Fixed entry. 5. task check; commit; push; PR.
narratives.Urgency: Low-medium severity per the issue. Adoption blocker only for the cold-start case on freshly-opened PRs with no fixes yet; routine push-driven review cycles are already safe. Worth landing now while it is fresh and the cohort is open; small change with no design risk.

items.title: Add `~` re-fetch rule + `⊗` no-single-fetch-exit rule to Phase 2 Step 1

narrative.Action: Insert verbatim per the issue body's 'Proposed Fix' block. Anchor: skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md, current Phase 2 Step 1 (lines ~77-100; verify before edit).

Short pointer to user rule `KthiAoZEVMVffm0rv4vxQ6` and templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md (which already handles this case in its loop body) so future readers see the deft-internal version is the canonical source.
Regex-assert the new `~` rule presence, the `⊗` rule presence, and the canonical phrasing tokens. Mirror the pattern in tests/content/test_skills.py.

Per [AXIOM] strongest-applicable-layer: the rule body lives in the skill, but a one-line lessons.md entry pointing at it is consistent with the existing pattern (e.g. PR #652 / Greptile CheckRun lesson). Optional; agent's call.
fix(skills/review-cycle): re-fetch after a delay when the initial dual-source pass returns no bot review on the current HEAD; closes the cold-start false-clean gap (#796).
